chosen_action.title: Prefer the truthful Codeberg-first primary publisher packet over generic manual follow-through assets

why_this_action.summary: At 2026-05-25 17:26 the selector still chose manual_outreach_asset_follow_through even though the execution board and current packet state already exposed a stronger truthful Codeberg-first primary publisher packet. That drift would keep wasting slots on placeholder follow-through instead of the highest-leverage available packet.
shared_findings_used: agents/marketing/logs/distribution_lane_latest.json: lane had drifted to manual_outreach_asset_follow_through at 2026-05-25T17:26:37+02:00
drafts/marketing_execution_board_latest.md: board already surfaced a do-now primary-repo-flat publisher contact packet for TIMEWELL, Toolradar, Morph

agents/marketing/logs/marketing_2026-05-25_163800_primary_packet_followthrough_truth_repair.json: prior repair explicitly said to escalate if a lower-priority manual outreach asset still outranked the current primary packet
agents/marketing/logs/marketing_workflow_audit_latest.json: Codeberg movement remains the primary success gate while same-family publisher overlap blocks net-new bursts

result.summary: Patched the selector so a primary-repo-flat follow-through asset mapped to the current handoff packet wins over generic manual outreach follow-through. Added regression coverage and verified the live selector now returns primary_repo_flat_contact_handoff_packet for the 2026-05-25 17:27 checkpoint.

live_selector_check.reason: The active repair window still blocks another net-new publisher burst, but a current Codeberg-first publisher contact packet already exists for fresh primary-repo-flat targets; reuse that packet as the truthful follow-through surface instead of stalling behind another measurement hold or churn guard.

replacement_condition: If a future run still chooses manual_outreach_asset_follow_through while the first manual asset is the primary-repo-flat packet, escalate into executor/board priority repair instead of logging another placeholder follow-through.
